name: test-app
comment_durable-functions: This app is wired to the IN-REPO durable-functions (compat) package and core @microsoft/durabletask-js via file: references, so the E2E suite exercises the on-branch build. Build the in-repo packages first (npm run build -w durable-functions from the repo root), then run npm install here. The @microsoft/durabletask-js override is REQUIRED: the compat package depends on @microsoft/durabletask-js 0.4.0, which is not yet published on npm — it is the on-branch core version that introduces the Functions worker helpers (processOrchestratorRequest/processEntityBatchRequest). Without the override, npm could not resolve the unreleased 0.4.0 (or would fall back to a stale published core), and the worker path would fail at runtime.

scripts.prestart: npm run clean && npm run build
